coredata.dc:title: Eigen spectra and wave functions of the massless Dirac fermions under the nonuniform magnetic fields in graphene
coredata.prism:publicationName: Physica E: Low-dimensional Systems and Nanostructures
coredata.dc:creator: Eshghi, M.
Mehraban, H.
Azar, I. Ahmadi
coredata.dc:description: In this research, firstly, by using the new form of Dirac-Weyl equation and the series method with submitting more suitable details, the energy spectrum and wave functions of the massless Dirac fermions are calculated under the inhomogeneous and q-deformed spatially magnetic fields. Although, we discussed about the results of the energy levels, further, we obtained the wave function as the Hessenberg determinant with calculating the elements of it as exact. On the other hand, by using the Mellin-Barnes integral representation and Hurwitz zeta function, we have achieved the thermodynamic physical quantities of the Dirac-Weyl fermions in the absence of a magnetic field for inside of the graphene quantum dot. Finally, our numerical results for the wave functions and probability densities are presented too.
coredata.dcterms:subject: Graphene
Dirac-Weyl equation
q-deformed magnetic field
Thermodynamic properties

xocs:meta.xocs:srctitle: Physica E: Low-dimensional Systems and Nanostructures
xocs:meta.xocs:articleinfo: articleinfo articletitlenorm authfirstinitialnorm authfirstsurnamenorm cid cids contenttype copyright crossmark dateloaded dateloadedtxt datesearch datesort dateupdated dco docsubtype doctype doi eid ewtransactionid hubeid indexeddate issn issnnorm itemstage itemtransactionid itemweight openaccess openarchive pg pgfirst pglast pii piinorm pubdateend pubdatestart pubdatetxt pubyr sortorder srctitle srctitlenorm srctype ssids alllist content subj subheadings suppl tomb volfirst volissue volumelist webpdf webpdfpagecount yearnav figure body mmlmath acknowledge affil articletitle auth authfirstini authfull authkeywords authlast primabst ref

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: The potential for four different values of inside magnetic fields.
The potential for four different values of outside magnetic fields.
The wave function for two different values of inside magnetic fields.
Corresponding probability density for two different values of inside magnetic fields.
The wave function for two different values of outside magnetic fields.
Corresponding probability density for two different values of outside magnetic fields.

head.ce:title: Eigen spectra and wave functions of the massless Dirac fermions under the nonuniform magnetic fields in graphene

ce:affiliation.ce:label: a
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Researchers and Elite Club, Central Tehran Branch, Islamic Azad University, Tehran, Iran

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: Researchers and Elite Club, Central Tehran Branch, Islamic Azad University

ce:affiliation.ce:label: b
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Physics Department, Semnan University, Semnan, Iran

ce:affiliation.ce:label: c
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Physics Department, Imam Hossein Comprehensive University, Tehran, Iran

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: Physics Department, Imam Hossein Comprehensive University

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: In this research, firstly, by using the new form of Dirac-Weyl equation and the series method with submitting more suitable details, the energy spectrum and wave functions of the massless Dirac fermions are calculated under the inhomogeneous and q-deformed spatially magnetic fields. Although, we discussed about the results of the energy levels, further, we obtained the wave function as the Hessenberg determinant with calculating the elements of it as exact. On the other hand, by using the Mellin-Barnes integral representation and Hurwitz zeta function, we have achieved the thermodynamic physical quantities of the Dirac-Weyl fermions in the absence of a magnetic field for inside of the graphene quantum dot. Finally, our numerical results for the wave functions and probability densities are presented too.

ce:section.ce:para: The study of physical phenomena is an important work with applying the magnetic fields. Inhomogeneous magnetic fields continue to play an important role in modern physics, from the historic Stern-Gerlach experiment [1] to the magnetic confinement of plasma in tokamaks [2] and the magnetic levitation of macroscopic objects [3]. Experimentally, these fields [4] may be created by various means, such as curving the membrane [5], or superconducting stripes on top of the 2D electron gas [6], or deposition of ferromagnetic microstructures [7]. Indeed, Lindvall et al., have successfully studied an experiment about weak localization in graphene in inhomogeneous magnetic fields [8,9].

On the other hand, graphene [10] is a single layer of carbon atoms arranged in two dimensional (2D) hexagonal honeycomb lattice. It is electric or magnetic properties and applied triggered a new research field in condensed matter and nuclear physics. Graphene has recently emerged as both a unique system for fundamental studies of condensed matter and quantum physics [11] and a fascinating building block for future devices in an area of post silicon [12–16]. Two of the most extraordinary properties of graphene are its absolute 2D (two-dimensionality) crystal structure and its Dirac particles. The charge carriers in the honeycomb lattice of graphene are described by the Dirac equation in the 2+1 space-time [17,18], rather than the usual Schrodinger's equation, it can be seen as a consequence of graphene crystal structure, which consists of two equivalent carbon sublattices [19]. Thus, the charged carriers in undoped graphene can be described as the massless relativistic fermions and are governed by the Dirac-like (Dirac-Weyl) equation. In this regard, much effort has been expounded on considerations of magnetic traps [9,20–29].

Also, quantum dots formed in semiconductor have been studied extensively. Electrons confined in usual semiconductor dots, with a typical size of a few hundreds of nanometers, are described by the Schrödinger equation. For example, we can see that Downing and Portnoi have investigated the motion of electrons confined under the inhomogeneous magnetic fields in the magnetic quantum dots by using the Schrödinger-Pauli Eq. [30].

But, we know that the physics of graphene quantum dots is very different than the usual semiconductor dots. We have two reasons: firstly, charge carriers in graphene are massless and obey the relativistic 2D Dirac-Weyl equation; secondly, the different configurations of the carbon atoms at boundaries of the dot affect the dot properties significantly. Also, for defining a graphene quantum dot, there are two basic methods; (i) the dots are defined through the application of electric and magnetic fields, (ii) the dots are defined by the actual geometry of the graphene layer. In this area, graphene quantum dot, Downing, et al. have presented exact analytical zero-energy solutions by using the Dirac-Weyl Eq. [31].

However, in this paper, the exact analytical expressions for the energy spectrum and wave function are calculated in the presence of nonuniform and q-deformed magnetic fields inside and outside of the graphene quantum dot by the generalized series method. We also submit details of method with solving this problem. Finally, we calculate some of the thermodynamic quantities for the given model. because, the study of the thermodynamic properties of semiconductor structure is very important, in particular, determining the behavior of the thermodynamic quantities such as: the specific heat, the entropy, the free energy, the mean energy, and the magnetization. In recent years, several researchers have been studied the thermodynamic properties of the various systems analytically. For example, see [32–38].

The organization of the present paper is as follows. In Section 2, we exhibit the Dirac-Weyl equation as suitable form and solve the Dirac-Weyl equation under the magnetic fields to obtain energy levels and wave functions with more details for clarity. Section 3 is devoted to discussions about calculation of the thermodynamic properties. Finally, in Section 4, we present our results as conclusion.

We consider the graphene plane sheet and construct the orthogonal curvilinear coordinate system
(
x
1
,
x
2
)
and the Cartesian coordinate system
(
y
1
,
y
2
)
in this sheet. We suppose the equations of the transformation
x

are called the Lamé coefficients of the coordinates
x

x

, respectively. We also suppose that a magnetic field which is perpendicular to the graphene sheet is applied. As we have known, behavior of the electrons obeys the massless relativistic Dirac-Weyl equation in the graphene sheet.

In Eq. (1), we express the components of the Hamiltonian matrix
H
ˆ
in a tensor form. When a physical expression or a physical law is expressed in a tensor form, it takes the same mathematical form in all the coordinate systems. This is a principle in theoretical physics, which is known as "the principle of covariance", it says that is covariant under arbitrary differentiable coordinate transformations
x
i
→
x
̅
i
. In Eq. (1), the Hamiltonian expression is in a tensor form in the 2-dimensional orthogonal curvilinear coordinate system
(
x
1
,

. This form of Hamiltonian is new, and we are the first people who started to use it in a research paper.

(
o
)
are the magnetic fields in inside and outside of the graphene quantum dot, the parameter
R
is the radius of the dot,
δ
is an adjustable positive and very small parameter, and q is a real positive parameter which is responsible for the deformation of the vector potential. We could not see such problem experimentally. But perhaps constructing such magnetic potentials in laboratories would be very simple. Because the above vector potential is the combination of Coulomb-like, exponential, and constant magnetic fields. Therefore, these potentials can be constructed simply in laboratory.

In
Figs. 1 and 2, we show the behavior of the vector potential as changing versus
ρ
for four different values of inside and outside magnetic fields. We choose the parameters as arbitrary.

In fact, in Figs. 1 and 2, we show the influence of the inside and outside magnetic fields on the vector potential. It is obvious that the vector potential approximately decreases with the increasing of
ρ
for different values of the inside and outside magnetic fields and with the state
n = 1
. We also see that the vector potential increases with the increasing of the inside and outside magnetic fields with fixed value of
ρ
. In Fig. 1, we see that the vector potential decreases exponentially, but, In Fig. 2, it decreases linearly and converges to the specific value.

−

)
]
< 0 .

By using the solutions (26) and (28), the wave function of the electron inside the graphene becomes

.

In Figs. 3 and 5, we show the behavior of the wave functions as a function of
ρ
for two different values of the inside and outside magnetic fields for
ρ < R
and
ρ > R
. We can see that the wave functions change with increasing inside and outside magnetic field effects by choosing the parameters as arbitrary. In fact, in these figures, the amplitude of wave function is decreasing with increasing value of inside and outside magnetic fields.

The corresponding probability densities have been shown in Figs. 4 and 6 for
ρ < R
and
ρ > R
, too. In here, in these figures, the amplitude of wave function with increasing of value inside and outside magnetic fields is increasing.

The thermodynamics properties of graphene and graphene nanoribbons have been studied under electric and magnetic modulations from the theoretical and experimental viewpoint (see [50–54]). In order to obtain all thermodynamic quantities of the pseudo-relativistic electrons system, we calculate the partition function of our system. The canonical partition function [55] of the electron inside and outside the graphene at the temperature
T
, is defined through the Boltzmann factor as
(37)
Z
i
(
o
)
(
β
)
=
∑
n
=
0
∞
exp
[
−
β
(
E
n
,

i
(
o
)
−
E
0 ,
m
i
(
o
)
)
]
,
where
β
=
1
/
k
B
T
,
k
B
is the Boltzmann constant and
E
0 , m
i
(
o
)
is the ground state energy of electron inside (outside) the graphene which is correspondent to the quantum number
n = 0
. To evaluate the partition function of inside the graphene quantum dot, substituting the energy spectrum formula – Eq. (18) – into the definition (37), yields the following equation:
(38)

(

)

e

)
,

Employing the residues theorem, for the two poles

Other thermodynamic properties of the system can be easily obtained from partition function (43). In fact, any other parameter that might contribute to the energy should also appear in the argument of

Also, the specific heat [55] can be found as

)
}
.

In this research, we studied the energy levels and wave functions of Dirac fermiouns under the influence of inhomogeneous distributions of the magnetic fields inside and outside in the graphene quantum dot. We showed that it can be found in an analytical closed form. In fact, we could submit a suitable analytically method for our work. It may be used in other similar problems. We can also calculate the elements of the Hessenberg determinant into the obtained wave function exactly. In the absence of an external magnetic field, the density of states, electronic density, gate voltage and thermodynamic quantities were obtained by using the obtained energy of Dirac fermions for inside the graphene quantum dot. Finally, some results of the wave functions and probability densities are shown in
Figs. 3–6.

ce:acknowledgment.ce:para: The authors would like to thank the referee for positive and invaluable suggestions which have greatly improved the manuscript.

ce:other-ref.ce:textref: V. Santos, R.V. Maluf, C.A.S. Almeida, arXive:1401.8051v2, 2014.
J.S. Ardenghi, P. Bechthold, E. Gonzalez, P. Jasen, A. Juan arXiv:1310.7154v1, 2013..

sb:title.sb:maintitle: A new development of the theory of the hypergeometric functions
